title: Corytholoma latifolium
taxonomy: Corytholoma latifolium is classified within the family Fabaceae. This plant is a species of legume, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants. Corytholoma latifolium is native to South Africa and is known for its broad leaves and ability to fix nitrogen from the air. It is often found growing in grasslands and savannas, where it can form dense stands. As a member of the Fabaceae family, Corytholoma latifolium is closely related to other legumes such as beans, peas, and clover.
